parsed_review.summary: CP00-187 is a descriptor-only Risk B pack binding the RP05.P06 permission substrate tail (RP05.P06.M03.S20-RP05.P06.M05.S15, 40 units) into secondary-workflow and permission/audit binding descriptors. Read-only inspection of the in-scope implementation (registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js), tests (model.test.js), the RP05 validator script, contract JSON, README, and the five pack deliverables confirms the production_ready determination is well-supported and not yet overclaimed. createMatterCorePermissionSubstrateWorkflowBindingDescriptor returns frozen data with 40 workflow-binding rows (3 Primary Implementation Slice + 22 Secondary Workflow Slice + 15 Permission And Audit Binding), exactly matching unit_count, the deliverable distribution (test 7 / security_audit 7 / implementation 16 / ui 8 / claude_review 2), and the micro_title distribution. Source-level search found no writeFile/fetch/child_process/database/network calls; all no-write flags are hard-coded false and validators reject any non-false value, including promotes_claude_to_final_approval and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation. The no-write/no-real-data boundary holds (synthetic tenant_rp05_synthetic fixtures only; descriptor leak guards verified via .every()). Authority boundary is preserved: the Claude review packet hard-sets read_only true, allowed_tools [Read,Grep,Glob], and claude_is_final_approval false; Hermes packets are static evidence/receipt descriptors with no product runtime embedding; production_ready_candidate is conditionally derived from validator results and explicitly a candidate, not a final or enterprise-trust claim. CP178+ hardening is intact: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1432-byte valid JSON, not 0-byte), forbidden_review_evidence enumerates auth failure / 0-byte stdout / malformed JSON / fenced JSON+prose / tool-call-shaped output / missing fields, and claude-review-result records zero falsely-accepted invalid attempts. Manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, adjudication, and claude-review-result all honestly report production_ready=false / pending, consistent with a pack awaiting this hardened review plus human adjudication. Unrelated dirty/untracked files are correctly fenced out of active scope. No P0 or P1 findings. One P3 informational note: the broader closeout validation suite and H05/C05 gates remain pending (expected, as this read-only review precedes final_closeout_validation), and must pass before the production_ready flip and commit. This review is advisory; human final approval remains required for the enterprise trust claim.

findings.title: Final closeout validation suite and H05/C05 gates still pending (expected pre-review state, not a deliverable defect)
findings.details: command-evidence.json records status=in_progress, gate_outcome=pending, and hermes_gate H05=pending, with only focused checks captured as passed (node --check on registry/service/validators/test/validator script, contract JSON parse, node --test on model.test.js, and npm run rp05:matter-core:validate). The broader validation commands listed in the review prompt (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, npm run closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, npm run spec:requirements:validate, npm run weighted:validate, npm run fullplan:validate, npm run goal:closeout:validate, npm run rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) and the H05/C05 gate outcomes are not yet recorded as passed. This is consistent with the hardened review sequence, in which this read-only Claude review precedes final_closeout_validation, and the pack correctly keeps production_ready=false across manifest, command-evidence, construction-inspection, adjudication, and claude-review-result, so it is not an overclaim. It is surfaced only as a closeout precondition for the human adjudicator.
findings.recommendation: Before flipping production_ready and committing, run and record the full validation command suite and the H05/C05 gate outcomes. Given the large number of unrelated dirty/untracked files preserved outside active scope, explicitly confirm git diff --check is clean (no whitespace/conflict-marker errors) over the active pack files.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational / non-blocking. Expected pre-review state inherent to the hardened sequence; tracked as a closeout precondition, not a defect in the CP00-187 deliverables. Does not block this review's PASS verdict.
